---
title: "Installation Guide"
---

## Detailed Installation

Complete guide for deploying Candidate Matcher from scratch.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│  DuckDB (Local) │────▶│  ETL Script      │────▶│  GitHub Pages   │
│  - jobs         │     │  (Weekly Cron)   │     │  (Static Site)  │
│  - candidates   │     │                  │     │                 │
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                                       │
                                                       ▼
                                                ┌─────────────────┐
                                                │  User Browser   │
                                                │  - Matching     │
                                                │  - localStorage │
                                                └─────────────────┘
```

---

## System Requirements

### For Deployment (One-time setup)
- Python 3.8+
- Git
- GitHub account
- DuckDB database (optional, sample data included)

### For Users
- Modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- JavaScript enabled
- localStorage allowed

---

## Step-by-Step Installation

### 1. Install Dependencies

```bash
# Install Python dependencies
pip install duckdb pandas

# Verify installation
python3 --version
pip list | grep duckdb
```

### 2. Clone Repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ezraair555/candidate-matching.git
cd candidate-matching
```

### 3. Prepare Database

#### Option A: Use Sample Data

```bash
# Sample data already included
ls -la sample.db  # Should exist
```

#### Option B: Create Your Database

Use the schema from [schema.qmd](schema.qmd):

```sql
-- Create jobs table
CREATE TABLE jobs (
    job_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    company TEXT,
    location TEXT,
    employment_type TEXT,
    experience_level TEXT,
    required_skills TEXT[],
    preferred_skills TEXT[],
    salary_min DECIMAL,
    salary_max DECIMAL,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active'
);

-- Create candidates table
CREATE TABLE candidates (
    candidate_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    first_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    last_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT,
    current_title TEXT,
    current_company TEXT,
    years_experience INTEGER,
    experience_level TEXT,
    skills TEXT[],
    availability TEXT
);
```

#### Option C: Generate Sample Data

```bash
python3 sample_data_generator.py
# Follow prompts to create test database
```

### 4. Export Data to JSON

```bash
python3 export_to_json.py \
    --duckdb-path /path/to/database.db \
    --github-repo .
```

Expected output:
```
✓ Exported 20 records from jobs → jobs.json
✓ Exported 50 records from candidates → candidates.json
✅ Export complete!
```

### 5. Initialize Git Repository

```bash
git init
git checkout -b main
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: Candidate Matcher"
```

### 6. Create GitHub Repository

#### Via GitHub CLI (Fastest)

```bash
# Install gh if not installed
# https://cli.github.com/

# Authenticate
gh auth login

# Create private repo
gh repo create candidate-matching --private --source=. --push

# Or create public repo
gh repo create candidate-matching --public --source=. --push
```

#### Via Web Interface

1. Go to https://github.com/new
2. Repository name: `candidate-matching`
3. Choose Public or Private
4. **Do NOT** initialize with README
5. Click **Create repository**
6. Follow commands shown to push existing code

### 7. Push to GitHub

```bash
# If not done automatically
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/candidate-matching.git
git push -u origin main
```

### 8. Enable GitHub Pages

1. Go to repo Settings → Pages
2. Source: Deploy from branch
3. Branch: main, Folder: /
4. Click **Save**
5. Wait 1-2 minutes

Your site is live at: `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/candidate-matching/`

---

## Automated Weekly Refresh

### Set Up Cron Job

```bash
crontab -e
```

Add this line:

```bash
0 6 * * 1 cd /home/lucas/.openclaw/workspace/candidate_matching && \
    python3 export_to_json.py \
        --duckdb-path /path/to/your/sigma_analytics.db \
        --github-repo . && \
    git add jobs.json candidates.json data_refresh_timestamp.json && \
    git commit -m "Weekly data refresh: $(date +\%Y-\%m-\%d)" && \
    git push origin main >> /tmp/candidate_cron.log 2>&1
```

### Test Cron Job

```bash
# Run manually first
/path/to/export_to_json.py --duckdb-path /path/to/db.db --github-repo .

# Check cron logs
grep CRON /var/log/syslog | tail -20
```

---

## Customization

### Change Theme Colors

Edit `styles.css`:

```css
:root {
    --primary: #0066cc;        /* Main blue */
    --primary-dark: #004c99;   /* Darker blue */
    --primary-light: #e6f0ff;  /* Light blue */
}
```

### Adjust Matching Weights

Edit `app.js`, find `calculateMatchScore()`:

```javascript
scores.overall_score = Math.round(
    (scores.skills_match_score * 0.40) +    // Change this
    (scores.experience_match_score * 0.25) + // Change this
    (scores.level_match_score * 0.15) +
    (scores.location_match_score * 0.10) +
    (scores.salary_match_score * 0.10)
);
```

### Add Custom Logo

Edit `index.html`:

```html
<div class="logo">
    <img src="your-logo.png" alt="Company Logo" height="40">
</div>
```

---

## Security Considerations

### For Private Repos

- GitHub Free: No Pages support for private repos
- GitHub Pro ($4/mo): Private repos with Pages ✅
- Alternative: Use Netlify or Vercel for private hosting

### Data Privacy

- Job/candidate data: Public if repo is public
- Notes: Stored locally in browser (never on server)
- No authentication: Anyone with URL can access (if public)

### Best Practices

1. Use private repo for sensitive data
2. Strip PII before export (email, phone)
3. Use HTTPS (automatic with GitHub Pages)
4. Regular backups of notes (export CSV weekly)

---

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] JSON files exported successfully
- [ ] Git repository initialized
- [ ] Code pushed to GitHub
- [ ] GitHub Pages enabled
- [ ] Site loads without errors
- [ ] Sample data displays correctly
- [ ] Job selection works
- [ ] Candidate matching works
- [ ] Notes can be added and saved
- [ ] Export to CSV/JSON works
- [ ] Weekly cron job scheduled

---

## Next Steps

- [Quick Start](quickstart.qmd) - If you skipped detailed steps
- [User Guide](user-guide.qmd) - Learn all features
- [Schema Reference](schema.qmd) - Database structure
- [Troubleshooting](documentation.qmd#troubleshooting) - Common issues
